architecture.name: Custom Headless Commerce
architecture.decision: Keep the storefront static and data-driven while commerce, payments, accounts, fulfillment, and provenance remain modular adapters.
architecture.rationale: KND product details will stay flexible until late launch. A custom headless model lets the catalog change quickly without forcing the brand into a fixed Shopify theme or a premature app backend.
architecture.futureCmsProvider: Optional headless CMS after product operations stabilize

layers.label: Catalog Source
layers.purpose: Products, variants, imagery, pricing placeholders, availability, and bundle rules.

layers.label: Commerce Adapter
layers.purpose: Cart rules, request/reserve/checkout modes, checkout provider settings, and order intake contract.

layers.label: Operational Ledger
layers.purpose: Drops, inventory, archive records, attestation, fulfillment states, accounts, and unit provenance.

layers.label: Checkout Provider
layers.purpose: Accept payment only when prices, tax, shipping, returns, and fulfillment rules are locked.

request.systemAction: Create an inquiry record without reserving inventory

reserve.systemAction: Create a reservation against a planned drop or inventory pool

checkout.systemAction: Create cart, send to checkout provider, then reconcile order and fulfillment events

launchPhases.name: Concept shop
launchPhases.description: Products render from data, with request and reserve actions instead of final checkout.

launchPhases.name: Private drop
launchPhases.description: Inventory pools, prices, reservation intake, and fulfillment rules lock for selected products.

launchPhases.name: Checkout
launchPhases.description: Stripe checkout adapter receives cart lines and returns order events for reconciliation.

launchPhases.name: Accounts and provenance
launchPhases.description: Customers can view orders, claimed units, family sizing, and attestation records.
